players.injury_note: Tatum (Achilles) could make his return to the lineup Friday against Dallas, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Bagley is out for Thursday's game against Orlando.
Irving (knee) will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, ESPN's Shams Charania reports.
Lively successfully underwent surgery on his right foot Sunday.
Williams (quadriceps) is out for Thursday's game against the Magic.
Salaun is out for Friday's game against the Heat due to a left calf strain.
Fontecchio has a left groin strain and will not play Thursday against the Nets.
Jovic (back) is out for Thursday's game against the Nets.
Powell is week-to-week with a right groin strain, per Anthony Chiang of the Miami Herald.
The NBA placed Rozier on immediate leave from the Heat on Thursday, ESPN's Shams Charania reports.
The Rockets announced Wednesday that Adams has undergone season-ending left ankle surgery, per Shams Charania of ESPN.
Finney-Smith (ankle) won't play in Thursday's game against the Warriors, Lachard Binkley of SI.com reports.
Tate has been diagnosed with a Grade 2 MCL sprain in his right knee and will be re-evaluated in two weeks, Vanessa Richardson of Space City Home Network reports.
VanVleet suffered a torn ACL and may be out for the entire 2025-26 campaign, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Avdija is doubtful for Friday's game against the Rockets due to lower-back injury management.
Lillard (Achilles) defeated Devin Booker and Kon Knueppel in the final round of Saturday's 2026 State Farm 3-Point Contest, taking home his third title.
Murray (illness) is listed as questionable for Friday's game against the Rockets.
Sharpe (calf) will be re-evaluated in 4-to-6 weeks, per Sean Highkin of Rip City Radio 620 Portland.
Gordon (hamstring) is targeting a return Friday against the Knicks, Sam Amick of The Athletic reports.
Johnson (ankle) has been ruled out for Thursday's game against the Lakers, Brendan Vogt of TheDNVR.com reports.
Jones (shoulder) has been ruled out for Thursday's game against the Lakers.

Watson has been ramping up his basketball activities as he continues to recover from a right hamstring strain and is slated to be re-examined later this week, Brett Siegel of ClutchPoints.com reports.
Hart (back) is questionable for Friday's game against the Nuggets.
McBride will undergo surgery for a core muscle injury and may be sidelined until the playoffs, James L. Edwards III of The Athletic reports.
Brooks' timetable for his broken left hand is 4-to-6 weeks, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Goodwin (calf) will remain on the inactive list for Thursday's game against the Bulls.
Williams will be re-evaluated in 2-to-3 weeks after being diagnosed with a stress reaction in the third metatarsal of his left foot, Shane Young of the Suns' official site reports.
Murray (reconditioning) is out for Thursday's game against the Kings.
Barnes (ankle) is out for Thursday's game against the Pistons.
Ingram has signed a two-way contract with the Spurs, Michael Scotto of USA Today reports.
Jones Garcia underwent ankle surgery Wednesday and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, Tom Orsborn of the San Antonio Express-News reports.
Plumlee (reconditioning) is out for Thursday's game against the Pistons.
Beal will undergo season-ending surgery on his left hip, ESPN's Shams Charania reports.
Collins (neck) won't travel with the Clippers for their upcoming two-game road trip, Tomer Azarly of ClutchPoints.com reports.
Garland is out for Friday's game against the Spurs due to left toe injury management.
Niederhauser has been diagnosed with a Lisfranc injury in his right foot and will require surgery. He's been ruled out for the remainder of the 2025-26 season, Joey Linn of SI.com reports.
Ayton left Thursday's game against Denver due to left knee soreness, Dan Woike of The Athletic reports.

James suffered a left elbow injury during the fourth quarter and was unable to finish Thursday's game against Denver, Dave McMenamin of ESPN.com reports. He ended with 16 points (7-11 FG, 0-2 3Pt, 2-2 FT), five rebounds, eight assists, three steals and one block in 34 minutes.
Kleber (back) has been ruled out for Thursday's game against the Nuggets, Trevor Lane of LakersNation.com reports.
Furphy was diagnosed with a torn right ACL on Monday and will miss the remainder of the season, Michael Scotto of USA Today reports.

Haliburton said Tuesday in his appearance on the "Mind the Game" podcast that he has resumed taking part in 3-on-3 and 4-on-4 work on the court while he continues to recover from right Achilles surgery.
Nembhard is probable for Friday's game against the Lakers due to low back and neck soreness.
Nesmith is probable for Friday's game against the Lakers due to a right ankle and lower back sprain.
Siakam (wrist) is listed as probable for Friday's game against the Lakers.
Zubac (ankle) has been ruled out for Friday's game against the Lakers.
